title: Regenerate full-stack DuckDuckGo search application

requirements.text: Implement a Motoko backend actor that performs HTTP outcalls to the DuckDuckGo API (https://api.duckduckgo.com/?q={query}&format=json) to fetch search results, parses and returns the JSON response, and handles HTTP errors gracefully with descriptive error messages.
acceptanceCriteria: Actor exposes a search function that accepts a query string and returns a result type containing either the raw JSON string or an error message
HTTP outcall is made to DuckDuckGo API with the URL-encoded query parameter
Failed HTTP requests return a descriptive error string rather than trapping
Successful responses return the raw JSON body

requirements.text: Implement the useSearch custom hook that calls the backend actor's search function, URL-encodes the query, validates the JSON response, extracts meaningful error messages from ICP canister traps, and exposes loading, error, and results state to consuming components.
acceptanceCriteria: Hook accepts a query string and returns { results, isLoading, error, search }
Query string is URL-encoded before being sent to the backend
JSON response is validated and parsed; invalid JSON surfaces a user-friendly error
ICP canister trap messages are extracted and displayed as readable errors
Loading state is true while the backend call is in flight

requirements.text: Implement the SearchInterface component that renders a search form, handles submission, displays loading/error/empty states, flattens DuckDuckGo topic groups into a flat results list, and opens result links in the InAppBrowser overlay component.
acceptanceCriteria: Search form has a text input and submit button
A loading spinner or indicator is shown while results are loading
An error message is displayed when the search fails
An empty state message is shown when no results are returned
DuckDuckGo RelatedTopics groups are flattened into a single list of result items
Clicking a result opens the link inside the InAppBrowser overlay

requirements.text: Implement the InAppBrowser component as a fullscreen modal overlay that displays an external URL in an iframe, includes a header bar with the URL, a loading spinner while the iframe loads, a close button to dismiss the overlay, and a fallback link to open the URL in a new tab.
acceptanceCriteria: Modal covers the full viewport when open
Iframe loads the provided URL
A loading spinner is shown until the iframe fires its onLoad event
A close button dismisses the overlay
A fallback 'Open in new tab' link is always visible in the header bar

requirements.text: Apply a cohesive dark-themed visual design across the entire application using a dark background with high-contrast foreground text, clean sans-serif typography, and a minimal card-based layout for search results. Define OKLCH-based CSS custom properties for the color system and ensure light/dark theme tokens are consistent throughout.
acceptanceCriteria: Global CSS defines OKLCH custom properties for background, foreground, and UI component color tokens
Tailwind config maps CSS variables to semantic color tokens
Search results are rendered in visually distinct cards
Typography uses a clean sans-serif font
The layout is centered and responsive

constraints: All Motoko logic must remain in the single backend/main.mo actor file
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui
nonGoals: User authentication or Internet Identity login flows
